summary: Pegloticase is indicated for refractory chronic gout due to its mechanism as a urate-lowering therapy targeting elevated serum uric acid levels that lead to monosodium ureturate deposition. It is primarily prescribed when conventional treatments fail, making it effective in managing severe cases of chronic gout with persistent symptoms despite standard care.
